rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE ROBERTS VS. SWANROCK INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 11, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANTs COLIN SWAN, JOEL FRANGQUIST and SWANROCK, INC. DBA SKYTECH SOLAR's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Swanrock Inc., Colin Swan, and Joel Frangquist's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with 20 days leave to amend as to the Sixth and Ninth Causes of Action. "A plaintiff alleging unfair business practices under these statutes must state with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the statutory elements of the violation." (Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 619.) Plaintiff Roberts fails to highlight the precise statutes violated in the body of the Sixth Cause of Action. She also fails to expressly pray for restitution and/or an injunction. The prior references to statutory violations at paragraphs 44-45 do not make it clear that they are the basis for the unlawful business practice cause of action. Paragraph 64 should be deleted as well because plaintiff admits that she is only suing on her own behalf and not on behalf of others. If she were suing on behalf of others, she would need to allege facts establishing her compliance with Code Civ. Proc. § 382. Plaintiff also has 20 days leave to amend the Ninth Cause of Action for Intentional Misrepresentation. "The specificity requirement means a plaintiff must allege facts showing how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were made, and, in the case of a corporate defendant, the plaintiff must allege the names of the persons who made the representations, their authority to speak on behalf of the corporation, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when the representation was made." (West v. JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 780, 793.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/acm

rulings.Case Title: RATTANA KIM ET AL VS. BOB TANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Of Defendant Bob Tang
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 11, 2018, Line 10, INTERVENOR SOKNY KOY's Motion To Set Aside Default Of Defendant Bob Tang Motion of Intervenor Sokny Koy to set aside default of defendant Bob Tang is GRANTED. Pursuant to Evid. Code § 647, use of a California registered process server affects the burden of producing evidence of improper service and there is a rebuttable presumption that service was proper. See Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1441-1442. Plaintiff used a California registered process server and the rebuttable presumption applies. The uncontroverted declaration of Intervenor Koy establishes that she has not seen her former husband, Bob Tang, since May 2017. In addition, the uncontroverted declaration of Chhay Ngy establishes that he was the party who was personally served on April 22, 2018, not defendant Bob Tang. The default is hereby vacated pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. § 473(d). (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544, [" a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void."]). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/acm

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO BARAJAS VS. SHI YUAN KUANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 11, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT SHI KUANG MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Defendant Shi Yuan Kuang's motion to quash summons is DENIED. Defendant Kuang fails to present any evidence supporting his motion. Kuang's motion set aside request for entry for default judgement, however, is GRANTED. Kuang's uncontroverted declaration support this court's finding that Kuang is unable to read or speak English and based on that, he was unaware that he was served with a summons. The declaration of Kuang's attorney supports this finding. There is sufficient evidence of excusable neglect presented. Code Civ. Proc., § 473 (b). Setting aside the default also comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits especially where, as here, Plaintiff asserts no legal prejudice from granting the motion. Defendant Kwang must file his answer to the complaint no later than September 19, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Kuang is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/acm

rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO BARAJAS VS. SHI YUAN KUANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 11, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANTs SHI KUANG and ALLISON WONG's Motion To Quash Summons Defendant Alison Wong's motion to quash summons is GRANTED. Pursuant to Evid. Code § 647, use of a California registered process server affects the burden of producing evidence of improper service and there is a rebuttable presumption that service was proper. See Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1441-1442. Plaintiff used a California registered process server and the rebuttable presumption applies. In her declaration, Wong states that she resided with a couple in their sixties and no person fitting the description on the proof of summons resided with her at the relevant time. Further, she affirms that she first became aware of the lawsuit when contacted by the attorney for Progressive Insurance. This evidence rebuts the presumption of proper service. No evidence has been presented by Plaintiff showing that the person served was a member of Wong's household. (See Fernandes v. Singh (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 932, 941, n.6, ["a declaration of non-service if credited by the trial court can rebut the presumption of proper service."]). The requirements of Code Civ. Proc., § 415.20 (b) have not been satisfied as to Defendant Wong. The default as to Wong is hereby vacated pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. § 473(d). (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544, [" a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void."]). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/acm

rulings.Case Title: EQUITAS BIO/PHARMA SOLUTIONS , LLC. VS. HISCOX INSURANCE COMPANY INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 11, 2018, Line 11, PLAINTIFF EQUITAS BIO/PHARMA SOLUTIONS , LLC.'s Motion To Seal Complaint Plaintiff's motion to seal portions of complaint pursuant to CRC 2.550(d) is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The Court grants the motion only for the items listed in paragraph 6 at page 3 of the Declaration of Third Party CEO. As to the remaining portions of the complaint, the request is denied. The Court is unable to make the specific findings required under CRC 2.550(d) for those portions of the complaint. Plaintiff is directed to draft a new order consistent with the tentative ruling. The Court grants the request for an order sealing the unredacted declaration of the Third Party CEO. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
